file.content:
---
title: "Engineering Intelligence, Shipped"
description: "The first Lightfast release: a neural pipeline, AI chat, four source integrations, a public API, TypeScript SDK, and MCP server."
keywords: ["lightfast", "engineering intelligence", "neural pipeline", "entity extraction", "ai chat", "github integration", "vercel integration", "linear integration", "sentry integration", "developer tools", "api", "sdk", "mcp"]
canonicalUrl: "https://lightfast.ai/changelog/2026-03-26-initial"
ogTitle: "Engineering Intelligence, Shipped"
ogDescription: "A neural pipeline that turns engineering events into searchable, connected knowledge you can query with AI."
noindex: false
nofollow: false
authors:
  - name: "Jeevan Pillay"
    url: "https://lightfast.ai"
    twitterHandle: "@jeevanpillay"
publishedAt: "2026-03-26T12:00:00Z"
updatedAt: "2026-04-08T12:00:00Z"
version: "v0.1.0"
type: "feature"
featuredImage: "/images/changelog/v010-hero.png"
tldr: "Lightfast v0.1.0 ships a neural pipeline that extracts entities and relationships from GitHub, Vercel, Linear, and Sentry — then lets you query it all with AI."
faq:
  - question: "What sources does Lightfast connect to in v0.1.0?"
    answer: "Lightfast connects to GitHub, Vercel, Linear, and Sentry via OAuth. Events flow in real-time through webhooks and are automatically processed by the neural pipeline."
  - question: "How does the neural pipeline work?"
    answer: "Every incoming event is validated, scored for significance, and decomposed into entities like PRs, deployments, issues, and endpoints. Entities are linked into a relationship graph and embedded into a vector store for semantic search."
  - question: "Can I access Lightfast programmatically?"
    answer: "Yes. Lightfast ships with a public REST API, a TypeScript SDK (lightfast on npm), and an MCP server (@lightfastai/mcp) for AI agent integration."
---

Here's everything in v0.1.0.

![Lightfast v0.1.0](/images/changelog/v010-hero.png)

## Neural Pipeline

The core of Lightfast. Every webhook event flows through four stages:

1. **Ingest** — validates webhook signatures, transforms provider-specific payloads into a canonical format, and stores a delivery log.
2. **Extract** — scores each event for significance, then decomposes it into up to 50 entities: PRs, deployments, issues, endpoints, branches, configs, and more. Each entity is deduplicated and tracked across occurrences.
3. **Graph** — resolves relationships between co-occurring entities and inserts edges with confidence scores. Entities that appear together in the same events become connected.
4. **Embed** — builds a narrative summary for each entity (identity, recent activity, relationships), generates a vector embedding, and upserts it into the search index.

No configuration required. Connect a source, and the pipeline handles everything from there.

{/* TODO: pipeline diagram or screenshot — apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-neural-pipeline.png */}

## AI Explore

Ask questions about your engineering infrastructure and get answers grounded in your actual data.

Explore is a streaming AI interface that searches your entity graph, retrieves relevant context from the vector store, and responds with cited, markdown-formatted answers. You can see exactly which entities and events informed each response through collapsible reasoning traces and tool call cards.

{/* TODO: screenshot of AI Explore — apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-ai-explore.png */}

## Source Connections

Connect GitHub, Vercel, Linear, and Sentry in seconds. Each integration uses OAuth with secure token storage — GitHub via App installation tokens, others via standard OAuth 2.0 flows.

Once connected, events flow in real-time through webhooks. Each source shows its status, active resources, and event subscriptions. You choose which repos, projects, and resources to track.

{/* TODO: screenshot of sources page — apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-sources.png */}

## Entities

A real-time browser for every entity extracted from your engineering events — repositories, pull requests, deployments, issues, users, endpoints, and more.

Filter by category, search by name, and watch new entities appear live as events are processed. Each entity links to a detail view showing its full metadata and event timeline.

Powered by server-sent events — no polling, no refresh.

{/* TODO: screenshot of entities list — apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-entities.png */}

## Events

A real-time feed of every event from your connected sources. Filter by provider, search by content, and scope by time range. New events stream in as they arrive.

{/* TODO: screenshot of events feed — apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-events.png */}

## Public API

A contract-first REST API with interactive documentation. Search entities with semantic reranking, or integrate Lightfast into your own tools and workflows.

Dual authentication: API keys for programmatic access, session tokens for browser-based flows.

{/* TODO: screenshot of API docs — apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-api-docs.png */}

## SDK & MCP Server

The TypeScript SDK (`lightfast` on npm) gives you typed access to the full API from any Node.js environment. The MCP server (`@lightfastai/mcp`) exposes the same capabilities to AI agents and tools that support the Model Context Protocol.

```bash
npm install lightfast
```

## API Keys

Create, rotate, and revoke org-scoped API keys from the dashboard. Keys are hashed at rest and shown only once on creation. Rotate atomically — the old key is revoked and a new one is issued in a single operation.

## Improvements

- **Real-time updates** — entities and events stream live via SSE across all views
- **Jobs dashboard** — monitor background workflows with live status, duration, and error details
- **Responsive sidebar** — collapsible navigation with mobile support
- **Platform-aware shortcuts** — Cmd on macOS, Ctrl on Windows and Linux
- **Command palette** — navigate anywhere or search entities with Cmd+K

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so m…
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep…
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/…
- claude-api: Build Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks to use the Claude API, Anthropic SDKs, or Managed Agents (`/v1/agents`, `/v1/sessions`); or asks to add a Claude feature (prompt caching, ad…
- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like "how do I do X", "find a skill for X", "is there a skill that can...", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for f…
- lightfast-sdk: Use Lightfast tools to search across team knowledge and interact with connected providers
(GitHub, Linear, Sentry, Vercel, Apollo). Triggers when the user asks about errors, issues,
PRs, deployments, or wants to search across their dev tools. Also t…
- lightfast-inngest: Inspect Inngest function runs, debug failures, send events, and invoke functions via the
local Inngest dev server MCP. Triggers when the user asks about background jobs, workflow
runs, failed steps, event delivery, or wants to invoke/test Inngest fu…
- lightfast-db: Query and inspect the Postgres database schema and data. Triggers when the user asks about
tables, columns, rows, database schema, wants to run a SELECT query, or needs to understand
the data model. Read-only — cannot modify data.
- turborepo: Turborepo monorepo build system guidance. Triggers on: turbo.json, task pipelines,
dependsOn, caching, remote cache, the "turbo" CLI, --filter, --affected, CI optimization, environment
variables, internal packages, monorepo structure/best practices,…
- implement_plan: Implement technical plans from thoughts/shared/plans with verification
- create_plan: Create detailed implementation plans through interactive research and iteration
- validate_plan: Validate implementation against plan, verify success criteria, identify issues
- commit: Create git commits with user approval and no Claude attribution
- oneshot_merge: Commit changes, create PR, and merge into main in one shot
- improve_plan: Adversarial review of implementation plans — find weaknesses, kill code smells, improve DX
- research_codebase: Document codebase as-is with thoughts directory for historical context

{
  "name": "@repo/app-remotion",
  "license": "FSL-1.1-Apache-2.0",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "private": true,
  "type": "module",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types": "./src/index.ts",
      "default": "./src/index.ts"
    }
  },
  "scripts": {
    "clean": "git clean -xdf .cache .turbo node_modules",
    "dev": "npx tsx src/watch.ts",
    "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
    "studio": "npx remotion studio src/index.ts",
    "render:all": "npx tsx src/render.ts",
    "render:video": "npx tsx src/render.ts --only video",
    "render:stills": "npx tsx src/render.ts --only stills"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@vendor/remotion": "workspace:*",
    "clsx": "catalog:",
    "react": "19.2.4",
    "react-dom": "19.2.4",
    "tailwind-merge": "catalog:"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@remotion/cli": "^4.0.434",
    "@repo/typescript-config": "workspace:*",
    "@repo/ui": "workspace:*",
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "catalog:tailwind4",
    "@types/node": "catalog:",
    "@types/react": "catalog:",
    "css-loader": "^7.1.4",
    "postcss": "catalog:tailwind4",
    "postcss-loader": "^8.1.1",
    "style-loader": "^4.0.0",
    "tailwindcss": "catalog:tailwind4",
    "tsx": "^4.21.0",
    "typescript": "catalog:"
  }
}

content.content:
1	/**
2	 * Composition Manifest — Single Source of Truth
3	 *
4	 * To add a new composition:
5	 * 1. Create the component in src/compositions/<name>/<name>.tsx
6	 * 2. Add a COMPONENTS entry in Root.tsx
7	 * 3. Add the composition definition here with its output targets
8	 * 4. Run: pnpm render:all
9	 *
10	 * The manifest drives:
11	 * - Root.tsx registration (Composition/Still elements)
12	 * - Rendering (format, scale, codec)
13	 * - Distribution (auto-copy to destination directories)
14	 * - Post-processing (favicon.ico bundling)
15	 */
16	import type { RenderMediaOptions } from "@vendor/remotion/renderer";
17	
18	// ── Types ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
19	
20	interface OutputTarget {
21	  /** Destination directory (relative to monorepo root) */
22	  dest: string;
23	  /** Filename override (defaults to `${compositionId}.${format}`) */
24	  filename?: string;
25	  /** Output format */
26	  format: "png" | "webp" | "webm";
27	  /** For stills extracted from video compositions: which frame to capture */
28	  frame?: number;
29	  /** Render scale factor (default: 1) */
30	  scale?: number;
31	}
32	
33	interface StillComposition {
34	  /** Component name in the COMPONENTS registry in Root.tsx */
35	  component: string;
36	  height: number;
37	  outputs: OutputTarget[];
38	  props?: Record<string, unknown>;
39	  type: "still";
40	  width: number;
41	}
42	
43	interface VideoComposition {
44	  /** Component name in the COMPONENTS registry in Root.tsx */
45	  component: string;
46	  durationInFrames: number;
47	  fps: number;
48	  height: number;
49	  outputs: OutputTarget[];
50	  renderProfile: Pick<
51	    RenderMediaOptions,
52	    "codec" | "imageFormat" | "scale" | "everyNthFrame" | "numberOfGifLoops"
53	  >;
54	  type: "video";
55	  width: number;
56	}
57	
58	export type CompositionEntry = StillComposition | VideoComposition;
59	
60	interface PostProcess {
61	  /** Where to write the .ico */
62	  dests: string[];
63	  filename: string;
64	  /** Composition IDs whose outputs feed into this post-process */
65	  sources: string[];
66	  type: "ico";
67	}
68	
69	export interface CompositionManifest {
70	  compositions: Record<string, CompositionEntry>;
71	  postProcess: PostProcess[];
72	}
73	
74	// ── Manifest ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
75	
76	export const MANIFEST: CompositionManifest = {
77	  compositions: {
78	    // ── Blog Featured Images ───────────────────────────────────────
79	    "blog-featured-concentric": {
80	      type: "still",
81	      component: "BlogFeaturedConcentric",
82	      width: 1200,
83	      height: 630,
84	      props: {},
85	      outputs: [
86	        {
87	          format: "png",
88	          dest: "packages/app-remotion/out/featured",
89	          filename: "blog-featured-concentric.png",
90	          scale: 2,
91	        },
92	        {
93	          format: "webp",
94	          dest: "packages/app-remotion/out/featured",
95	          filename: "blog-featured-concentric.webp",
96	          scale: 2,
97	        },
98	      ],
99	    },
100	    "blog-featured-lissajous": {
101	      type: "still",
102	      component: "BlogFeaturedLissajous",
103	      width: 1200,
104	      height: 630,
105	      props: {},
106	      outputs: [
107	        {
108	          format: "png",
109	          dest: "packages/app-remotion/out/featured",
110	          filename: "blog-featured-lissajous.png",
111	          scale: 2,
112	        },
113	        {
114	          format: "webp",
115	          dest: "packages/app-remotion/out/featured",
116	          filename: "blog-featured-lissajous.webp",
117	          scale: 2,
118	        },
119	      ],
120	    },
121	    "blog-featured-base": {
122	      type: "still",
123	      component: "BlogFeaturedBase",
124	      width: 1200,
125	      height: 630,
126	      props: {},
127	      outputs: [
128	        {
129	          format: "png",
130	          dest: "packages/app-remotion/out/featured",
131	          filename: "blog-featured-base.png",
132	          scale: 2,
133	        },
134	        {
135	          format: "webp",
136	          dest: "packages/app-remotion/out/featured",
137	          filename: "blog-featured-base.webp",
138	          scale: 2,
139	        },
140	      ],
141	    },

229	      component: "BlogFeaturedDuo",
230	      width: 1200,

232	      props: {},
233	      outputs: [

content.text: Let me look at one of the existing blog featured compositions as a reference pattern.

input.prompt:
I need to understand the pattern used for blog featured image compositions in this Remotion project. 

Look at:
1. `/Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/blog-featured-base/` - read both files
2. `/Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/blog-featured-concentric/` - read both files
3. `/Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/blog-featured-trail/` - read both files
4. `/Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/app-remotion/src/styles.css` - the global styles

I want to know:
- What shared utilities/patterns do they use (e.g., shared background, color palette, cn utility)
- The component structure pattern (default export, props interface, etc.)
- How they use Tailwind CSS within Remotion
- The design language: colors, grid patterns, visual motifs used across compositions

Report the full code of blog-featured-base (as it's likely the simplest/foundational one) and summarize patterns from the others.

content.text:
I now have everything needed for a complete analysis. Here is the full breakdown.

---

## Analysis: Blog Featured Image Compositions

### Overview

All three `blog-featured-*` compositions follow an identical structural shell: a React functional component that defers Remotion rendering via `delayRender`/`continueRender` until Geist fonts are loaded, then returns an `<AbsoluteFill>` with a dark background. The visual differentiation between compositions is purely in what SVG content is rendered inside that `<AbsoluteFill>`. The design language is monochromatic (no hue — all colors are achromatic oklch), using the Lightfast Lissajous logo curve as the sole visual motif.

---

### Full Code: `blog-featured-base`

`/Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/blog-featured-base/blog-featured-base.tsx`

```tsx
import { AbsoluteFill, continueRender, delayRender } from "@vendor/remotion";
import type React from "react";
import { useEffect, useState } from "react";
import { ensureFontsLoaded } from "../landing-hero/shared/fonts";

export const BlogFeaturedBase: React.FC = () => {
  const [handle] = useState(() => delayRender("Loading fonts"));

  useEffect(() => {
    void ensureFontsLoaded()
      .then(() => continueRender(handle))
      .catch((err: unknown) => {
        console.error("Font loading failed:", err);
        continueRender(handle);
      });
  }, [handle]);

  return <AbsoluteFill className="bg-background" />;
};
```

This is the foundational shell — font loading + dark background, no SVG content. The `index.tsx` is a single re-export line in every composition.

---

### Shared Utilities and Patterns

#### Font Loading (`../landing-hero/shared/fonts.ts`)

Every composition imports `ensureFontsLoaded` from the same shared file at `/Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/app-remotion/src/compositions/landing-hero/shared/fonts.ts`.

The pattern is identical across all three:
- `delayRender("Loading fonts")` is called inside `useState` initializer (runs once, synchronously, before first render)
- `useEffect` calls `ensureFontsLoaded()`, then `continueRender(handle)` in both the `.then()` and `.catch()` — meaning render proceeds even if font loading fails

`ensureFontsLoaded` loads two weights of Geist (400 Regular and 500 Medium) via `loadFont` from `@vendor/remotion/fonts`, pointing at static `.woff2` files. A module-level `fontsLoaded` boolean prevents double-loading.

#### Brand Curve (`@repo/ui/lib/brand`)

`/Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/ui/src/lib/brand/lissajous.ts` is the canonical source for all visual geometry.

- `LOGO_CURVE = { a: 3, b: 2, delta: Math.PI / 2 }` — the Lightfast logo's Lissajous parameters
- `lissajousPath(size, padding, a, b, delta, steps?)` — returns a complete SVG `d` string with `M…L…Z`, centered in a square canvas of `size` px, with `padding` fraction reserved
- `lissajousPoints(a, b, delta, steps?)` — returns raw `[x, y]` pairs normalized to `[-1, 1]` (513 points by default at steps=512)

`blog-featured-concentric` uses `lissajousPath` + `LOGO_CURVE`. `blog-featured-trail` uses `lissajousPoints`.

---

### Component Structure Pattern

All three compositions follow the same structure:

1. Named export (PascalCase matching directory name): `export const BlogFeaturedBase: React.FC = () => {`
2. `index.tsx` re-exports the named component (no default exports anywhere)
3. No props interface — all compositions are `React.FC` with no generics/props
4. Canvas constants defined at module scope (`CANVAS_W = 1200`, `CANVAS_H = 630`, `CX`, `CY`)
5. Expensive geometry pre-computed in `useMemo` with empty dep array (runs once)
6. JSX root is always `<AbsoluteFill className="bg-background">` with SVG children inside

---

### Tailwind CSS Usage

`/Users/jeevanpillay/Code/@lightfastai/lightfast/packages/app-remotion/src/styles.css` uses Tailwind v4 (`@import "tailwindcss"`) with `@source` directives pointing at the compositions and lib directories.

The `@theme inline` block maps Tailwind color tokens to CSS custom properties (e.g., `--color-background: var(--background)`). The `:root` block then defines the actual values using `oklch()` — all with zero chroma (fully achromatic/greyscale):

| Token | oklch value | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--background` | `oklch(0.2178 0 0)` | Very dark grey — the canvas background |
| `--foreground` | `oklch(0.8853 0 0)` | Light grey — all SVG strokes and fills |
| `--muted-foreground` | `oklch(0.5999 0 0)` | Mid grey |
| `--border` | `oklch(0.329 0 0)` | Subtle border grey |

In practice, only two Tailwind classes appear in the composition files:
- `bg-background` — applied to every `<AbsoluteFill>`, resolves to `oklch(0.2178 0 0)`

All SVG elements use `stroke="var(--foreground)"` and `fill="var(--foreground)"` as inline style attributes directly (not Tailwind classes).

---

### Design Language

**Colors:** Strictly monochromatic. No hues at all. Dark background (~L 0.22) with light foreground (~L 0.89). Opacity is the only dimension used to create depth and hierarchy.

**Visual motif:** The Lightfast Lissajous curve (`a=3, b=2, δ=π/2` — a "pretzel" shape) is the exclusive visual element across all compositions. No typography, no grids, no other shapes.

**Canvas size:** Fixed at 1200×630 (standard OG image dimensions), centered at CX=600, CY=315.

**Rendering approaches per composition:**

- `blog-featured-base` — empty background only, the base layer with no curve drawn
- `blog-featured-concentric` — 7 concentric rings of the same Lissajous path at linearly interpolated sizes (`SIZE_MIN=100` to `SIZE_MAX=560`). Opacity inverts as size increases: innermost ring at `0.82`, outermost at `0.08`. Stroke weight grows from `1.2` to `1.8px`. A hairline crosshair (`±12px`, `opacity=0.12`) marks the canvas center. Outer rings render first so inner rings sit on top via SVG paint order.
- `blog-featured-trail` — a single instance of the Lissajous curve split into 120 segments. The first 70% (`TRAIL_FILL=0.7`) of the curve is the "active trail" with per-segment opacity following a `t^1.8` power curve (dim tail → bright head). The remaining 30% is a "ghost" at `opacity=0.06`. The head point gets a three-layer glow: outer halo (`r=7, opacity=0.12`), mid ring (`r=3.5, opacity=0.35`), bright core dot (`r=1.8, opacity=1.0`).

content.text:
Remotion Studio is running at **http://localhost:3000**. Open it and select **`changelog-v010-hero`** from the composition list.

Here's what the scaffold renders:

- **Center**: The Lightfast Lissajous curve (the logo motif) at 280px
- **Orbit**: 12 entity nodes arranged in an elliptical ring around the curve, connected by dashed lines to their nearest point on the curve — representing entities flowing into the neural pipeline
- **Orbital guide**: A faint ellipse showing the orbit path
- **Version label**: "v0.1.0" in the bottom-right corner

Everything follows the existing design language: monochromatic oklch palette, `bg-background` fill, SVG strokes using `var(--foreground)`, no color. The output targets are configured to render to `apps/www/public/images/changelog/v010-hero.png` (and `.webp`) at 2x scale — matching the `featuredImage` path in the changelog MDX.

Take a look in the studio and let me know what direction you want to take it.
